extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNS Dylan won a two-up sprint after DUMOULIN initiated the decisive selection early, then PINOT's attacks with 17km to go created the final winning group ahead of isolated GC favorites.
tactical_patterns: DUMOULIN forced tempo on penultimate climb despite Giro injury recovery, exploding early break and setting up later selections.
PINOT attacked at 17km out, creating 9-rider elite group that distanced BARDET, PORTE, MARTIN by 41 seconds through sustained aggression.
FROOME repeatedly bridged gaps but couldn't follow final accelerations, revealing limited explosive capacity despite diesel strength.
Group of BARDET/PORTE/MARTIN failed to organize chase despite having numbers, losing 41 seconds through hesitation on rolling finale.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: initiated race-splitting tempo on penultimate climb despite Giro knee injury, showing surprising recovery and willingness to animate
rider_signals.PINOT Thibault: positive: launched decisive selection with 17km attack, repeatedly accelerated to test rivals despite pre-race claims of merely testing form
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: made crucial bridge to winning group and countered effectively, showing elite positioning instincts on rolling terrain
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: visibly struggling to hold wheels in winning group, appeared to lack explosive response to accelerations
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: strong enough to bridge gaps but couldn't follow sharpest accelerations, diesel engine without top-end punch
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: missed critical PINOT split at 17km, then failed to organize chase group despite having PORTE/MARTIN present
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: caught behind decisive move, did most chase work but lost 41 seconds showing tactical isolation and physical limits
